predictions.forecast_value: Increase by approximately 100bps from December 2017 level (~1.25-1.50%), implying ~2.25-2.50%
predictions.source_quote: By the end of the year, Fed funds is expected to be 100bps from where it is today, but that will still leave it roughly 400bps below the pre-crisis peak.

predictions.forecast_value: No rate rise in 2018; first rate rise in 2019
predictions.source_quote: Based on its current timetable, that would point to the first rate rise in 2019.

predictions.forecast_value: Grind slightly higher but remain very low historically
predictions.source_quote: our base-case scenario is for longer-term yields to grind slightly higher, but to remain very low on a historical basis.
the Chinese authorities have recently tightened policy, which should serve to slow growth slightly in 2018, although it should remain in excess of 6%.
